ASSESSMENTS.title: Learner Self-reflection 1 – 24350 (v3) Identify, select, use and maintain portable power tools for BCATS projects
ASSESSMENTS.subtitle: Power sources, electrical safety, extension leads, overheating and ventilation

questions.text: What are the possible consequences of failing to disconnect a power tool from the power source before making adjustments to it?
questions.hint: See health and safety pages on accidental starting and the teacher answer guidance.

questions.text:
AC mains-powered tools
Advantages:
questions.hint: Accept any valid advantage of mains-powered tools.

questions.text:
AC mains-powered tools
Disadvantages:
questions.hint: Accept cord restriction, trip hazards, shock risk or similar.

questions.text:
Cordless power tools 
Advantages:
questions.hint: Accept portability, no cord, lower shock risk or similar.

questions.text:
Cordless power tools 
Disadvantages:
questions.hint: Accept battery life or lower power related answers.

questions.text:
Pneumatic tools 
Advantages:
questions.hint: Accept no electric shock risk, high power, lighter weight or similar.

questions.text:
Pneumatic tools 
Disadvantages:
questions.hint: Accept hose/air line, compressor, noise or compressed air hazards.

questions.text: Why should you never carry a power tool by the lead or unplug it by pulling on the lead?
questions.hint: Accept damage to wiring/lead leading to shock.

questions.text: What hazards are associated with laying extension leads directly on the ground?
questions.hint: Trip hazard is the key idea.

questions.text: What should you do if you find a kink in a lead?
questions.hint: Unwind/release and inspect the lead.

questions.text: What is likely to happen if you roll your leads up in a tight knot or ball?
questions.hint: Heat build-up/fire risk is the key idea.

questions.text: What could happen if you cut through a live extension lead?
questions.hint: Electric shock/electrocution is the key idea.

questions.text: What does smoke coming from a power tool, or one that smells of burning, indicate and what should be done to remedy the situation?
questions.hint: Overheating plus stop/cool/check vents/bit is enough.

questions.text: What are the effects of having clogged or blocked air vents on a tool?
questions.hint: Reduced ventilation causing overheating/motor damage.

ASSESSMENTS.title: Learner Self-reflection 2 – 24350 (v3) Identify, select, use and maintain portable power tools for BCATS projects
ASSESSMENTS.subtitle: Screwdriver bits, drill bits, drill selection and drill safety

questions.text: Why do you not use a poorly-fitting screwdriver bit when using a Electric Plane?
questions.hint: Accept slipping/stripping/injury/damage.

questions.text: What materials are the cheapest drill bits made of?
questions.hint: HSS / High Speed Steel.

questions.text: What is the disadvantage of using this type of drill bit?
questions.hint: Loses edge / becomes blunt.

questions.text: What is the most common type of quality general-purpose drill bit?
questions.hint: Accept tungsten carbide tipped or carbide bit.

questions.text: Describe the advantage of the centre-point drill bit for drilling timber.
questions.hint: Locates centre accurately and reduces wandering/skipping.

questions.text: What type of drill bit is needed to drill into concrete?
questions.hint: Masonry bit.

questions.text: What type of power drill do you need to drill through concrete?
questions.hint: Hammer drill / hammer action drill.

questions.text: You need to drill a hole in a narrow, confined space. What type of power drill is best to use?
questions.hint: Accept angle drill or compact cordless drill as reasonable school-level answer.

questions.text: What could happen if you try to hold small objects in your hands when drilling?
questions.hint: Movement/loss of grip/drop/injury is sufficient.

questions.text:
Identify 4 safety rules for when using a portable power drill.

	1.
questions.hint: Any valid drill safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify 4 safety rules for when using a portable power drill.

	2.
questions.hint: Any valid drill safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify 4 safety rules for when using a portable power drill.

	3.
questions.hint: Any valid drill safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify 4 safety rules for when using a portable power drill.

	4.
questions.hint: Any valid drill safety rule.

questions.text: When drilling a hole in a piece of metal using a large-sized bit, what is the possible injury to the operator if the drill jams?
questions.hint: Accept spinning material, bruising, cuts, wrist injury.

questions.text:
Identify THREE actions that can cause a drill bit to break.

	1.
questions.hint: Any valid cause such as jammed flutes, blunt bit, wrong angle, grabbing on exit.

questions.text:
Identify THREE actions that can cause a drill bit to break.

	2.
questions.hint: Any valid cause such as jammed flutes, blunt bit, wrong angle, grabbing on exit.

questions.text:
Identify THREE actions that can cause a drill bit to break.

	3.
questions.hint: Any valid cause such as jammed flutes, blunt bit, wrong angle, grabbing on exit.

questions.text: What can you do to avoid splintering the exit hole?
questions.hint: Backing/support timber, drill from both sides, ease pressure.

questions.text:
You have to drill a 38mm hole through a piece of 10mm MDF. What type of drill and drill bit should you choose? Give reasons for your choice:
Type of bit:
questions.hint: Accept Forstner/Fostner or hole saw.

questions.text:
You have to drill a 38mm hole through a piece of 10mm MDF. What type of drill and drill bit should you choose? Give reasons for your choice:
Reason:
questions.hint: Accept clean cut, suitable for MDF, avoid breakout/splintering.

ASSESSMENTS.title: Learner Self-reflection 3 – 24350 (v3) Identify, select, use and maintain portable power tools for BCATS projects
ASSESSMENTS.subtitle: Saws, jigsaws, reciprocating saws and circular saw safety

questions.text: What hazards are associated with using a power tool with a blunt cutter?
questions.hint: Accept overload/overheat, kickback/ejection, excessive sparks/noise.

questions.text: When using a jigsaw incorrectly, what is likely to happen if the blade were allowed to come out of the saw kerf on the up stroke?
questions.hint: Accept saw pushed back, blade damage or surface damage.

questions.text: Why is it important to have adequate clearance underneath the work for the jigsaw blade?
questions.hint: Accept avoiding cutting hidden items and avoiding blade bend/jam.

questions.text:
List THREE items of PPE that should always be worn when using a jig saw or a reciprocating saw.

	1.
questions.hint: Accept earmuffs, safety glasses/goggles, dust mask, gloves.

questions.text:
List THREE items of PPE that should always be worn when using a jig saw or a reciprocating saw.

	2.
questions.hint: Accept earmuffs, safety glasses/goggles, dust mask, gloves.

questions.text:
List THREE items of PPE that should always be worn when using a jig saw or a reciprocating saw.

	3.
questions.hint: Accept earmuffs, safety glasses/goggles, dust mask, gloves.

questions.text: The finer the set, the smoother the cut. True or false?
questions.hint: True.

questions.text: A round window is to be fitted into the side of a weatherboard house.  What type of saw would be best to cut the opening in the wall?
questions.hint: Accept jigsaw or reciprocating saw.

questions.text: What safety precautions should be taken before replacing a blade or making adjustments to a power saw?
questions.hint: Disconnect/unplug/remove battery.

questions.text: Why is it necessary to hold a Skill Saw firmly with both hands?
questions.hint: Kickback/catching/control.

questions.text: What are the potential consequences of using a saw with a faulty or missing lower guard?
questions.hint: Exposure to blade causing injury, especially in kickback.

questions.text: Why do you not force a circular saw’s cut speed?
questions.hint: Accept jamming/catching/kickback/blade exposure/burnout.

questions.text: What is likely to happen if you place a saw down on an exposed blade?
questions.hint: Accept travel/run unpredictably, blade damage, injury.

questions.text:
Identify and list SIX safety rules to follow when using Skill Saws.

	1.
questions.hint: Any valid circular saw safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify and list SIX safety rules to follow when using Skill Saws.

	2.
questions.hint: Any valid circular saw safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify and list SIX safety rules to follow when using Skill Saws.

	3.
questions.hint: Any valid circular saw safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify and list SIX safety rules to follow when using Skill Saws.

	4.
questions.hint: Any valid circular saw safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify and list SIX safety rules to follow when using Skill Saws.

	5.
questions.hint: Any valid circular saw safety rule.

questions.text:
Identify and list SIX safety rules to follow when using Skill Saws.

	6.
questions.hint: Any valid circular saw safety rule.

ASSESSMENTS.title: Learner Self-reflection 4 – 24350 (v3) Identify, select, use and maintain portable power tools for BCATS projects
ASSESSMENTS.subtitle: Router bits, router safety and burn marks

questions.text: Explain how to fit a 6.5mm shanked router bit in a 12.5mm chuck.
questions.hint: Use a 6.5 to 12.5 mm collet/sleeve/reducer.

questions.text: What is the advantage of a tungsten carbide bit over a HSS bit?
questions.hint: Keeps edge longer / less wear.

questions.text: What hazards are associated with using a tool that has an incorrectly fitted or loose cutter?
questions.hint: Cutter could be ejected and injure someone.

questions.text: Describe the effect of a faulty cutter head on a router.
questions.hint: Poor cut/burnishing/overload/motor damage.

questions.text: What would happen if a router bit struck a nail?
questions.hint: Accept bit damage plus hot metal/tungsten fragments risk.

questions.text: Once the cut is completed, what should you do with the router while you are waiting for the blade to stop spinning?
questions.hint: Turn off and keep under control; don't leave unattended.

questions.text: The surface that you are cutting is producing a charred or burnt finish. Why is it doing this and what can be done to stop it from happening?
questions.hint: Accept blunt/dirty bit or wrong speed/feed, and clean/replace/adjust.

ASSESSMENTS.title: Learner Self-reflection 5 – 24350 (v3) Identify, select, use and maintain portable power tools for BCATS projects
ASSESSMENTS.subtitle: Planers, nail guns, airless sprayers, concrete mixing and compressed air

questions.text: What objects sometimes found in timber will chip blades?
questions.hint: Accept nails, grit, concrete, stones or similar.

questions.text: What will happen if you place your fingers under a planer while it is going?
questions.hint: Finger injury/cutting is the key idea.

questions.text: Why should you wait until the blades have stopped before putting the planer down?
questions.hint: Accept travelling/moving across bench causing danger.

questions.text: Why should you never use your fingers to clean out a chip chute?
questions.hint: Accept hidden sharp waste/metal/blade edges.

questions.text: How do you prevent gouging at either end of timber when using a planer?
questions.hint: Accept angle, sacrificial blocks, or cut ends off after planing.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	1.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	2.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	3.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	4.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	5.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	6.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	7.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	8.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text:
List at least 5 safety rules for nail guns.

	9.
questions.hint: Any valid nail gun safety rule.

questions.text: What is the number one rule when using airless spray guns?
questions.hint: Do not let the gun tip contact skin/body.

questions.text: What is likely to happen if this rule isn’t followed?
questions.hint: High pressure injection of toxins/paint into bloodstream.

questions.text: What PPE must always be used when mixing concrete?
questions.hint: Gloves, dust mask/respirator, safety boots, safety glasses acceptable.

questions.text: Why is it important to never direct compressed air at any part of anyone’s body?
questions.hint: Accept eye injury or air embolism/bloodstream risk.

ASSESSMENTS.title: Learner Self-reflection 6 – 24350 (v3) Identify, select, use and maintain portable power tools for BCATS projects
ASSESSMENTS.subtitle: Sanding dust, angle grinder hazards and spark control

questions.text: What are the different methods for collecting dust when using electric sanders?
questions.hint: Accept dust bag/boot and vacuum/dust extractor.

questions.text: What will happen if you put too much pressure on a sander?
questions.hint: Motor stalls and may burn out.

questions.text: Why should you lay the sander on its side when you have finished sanding?
questions.hint: Prevent travel/movement across bench and accidental activation issues.

questions.text: What are the potential hazards when using an angle grinder near thinners, rags and other flammable materials?
questions.hint: Fire/ignition from sparks.

questions.text: When operating an angle grinder, what steps can be taken to manage or control the effects of sparks on other people?
questions.hint: Adjust guard, barrier, reposition work/self to direct sparks away.

questions.text: What are the dangers of using excessive force on the disc when using an angle grinder?
questions.hint: Disc can jam/break and pieces can be ejected.

questions.text: Why is it necessary never to apply pressure to the side of cutting discs (side loading) when operating an angle grinder?
questions.hint: Warping/cracking/shattering of disc.
